reasoning.A:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: My puzzle is 9 × 10 = 90, so Q1-A is certainly correct. In all previous games, everyone’s arithmetic has been accurate and we’ve never seen conflicting capsules, so Q2-B, Q3-C, and Q4-D are very likely correct as well. When I volunteer under these conditions, we’ve always escaped; when I didn’t (earlier game), we faced a random-death risk. Verifying would just cost a coin and still require a volunteer, without meaningfully improving safety, so I’ll volunteer using each question’s existing capsule.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Verify: Q4  
REASONING: I know my own answer for Q2 (52 + 31) is correct, so verifying Q2 is unnecessary. A and C have been consistently reliable in past games, whereas D is the only one known to have submitted a wrong capsule before, making Q4-D the weakest link. Spending 1 coin to verify Q4 either confirms D’s answer or creates a corrected alternative, increasing overall confidence in the password without changing my risk of random elimination if no one volunteers.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Pass  
REASONING: In every prior game, A has volunteered in round 1 using the single capsules (Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D), and all answers have been correct, ending the game immediately. Verifying another question this round wouldn’t affect whether someone volunteers or whether their chosen password is correct; it only helps if the game continues to later rounds, which so far has never happened. Verifying would therefore just cost me 1 coin with almost no benefit, and volunteering myself would add personal risk for no extra reward. Passing preserves my 4 coins while keeping essentially the same survival odds.
reasoning.D:
DECISION: Pass
REASONING: I am a dummy agent and always pass.

rounds.outcome:
🔍 Verifications completed:
  ✅ B agrees with Q4-D → now Q4-D+B (-1 coin: 4→3)

✅ A entered CORRECT password using capsules: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D+B
🎉 Everyone escapes! 🎉
